market_regime.description: Normal volatility - standard setups work well

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (5.90) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 3.4% move to $110.90 (weekly expected move: 13.7%).

thesis: ** HOOD is driven by perfect momentum (100) and strong trend persistence (85), suggesting a sustained directional move rather than a mean-reversion trap — critical in a normal VIX regime where momentum weights run at baseline 1.0x. This week is the right entry because COT speculative positioning is at the 13.7th percentile (extreme short), which per our methodology adds +2.5 contrarian bullish bias, and the stock has no earnings event to create binary risk. The thesis is invalidated if HOOD gaps down >2% on Monday open (gap-skip rule), or if broad risk-off sentiment intensifies — with cross-asset already showing 2 headwinds/0 tailwinds, any further deterioration (e.g., VIX push above 20) would undercut the directional bet.

market_regime.description: Normal volatility - standard setups work well

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (0.73) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 0.2% move to $22.27 (weekly expected move: 6.0%).

thesis: ** T is driven by exceptional trend persistence (95) and solid momentum (83) to the downside, indicating a well-established bearish trend rather than a one-day dip. The narrow-leadership breadth regime specifically hurts telecom — when SPY outperforms RSP, capital rotates away from yield-oriented defensives like T toward growth leaders. Kill condition: if T reclaims its SMA20 by Tuesday close or RSI drops below 30 (entering oversold territory where mean-reversion signals would conflict with the put thesis at 1.0x weight in normal VIX).

market_regime.description: Normal volatility - standard setups work well

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (5.10) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 1.0% move to $230.10 (weekly expected move: 5.6%).

thesis: ** MS is anchored by elite trend persistence (95) and strong momentum (90), with strike efficiency (75) suggesting clean execution at the 0.35 delta target. Tight credit spreads, while penalizing our macro edge multiplier broadly, are directly favorable for bank earnings expectations and credit book quality — this is a case where the macro headwind for the system is a micro tailwind for the name. The thesis dies if credit spreads widen sharply (HY OAS moving above 3.5% mid-week), which would reverse the financials-specific tailwind, or if VIX spikes above 25 where momentum weight drops to 0.7x and the call thesis loses its primary engine.
